assignment

2026-04-30

#Library

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(epiwraps)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(AnnotationHub)
  library(ensembldb)
  library(GenomicRanges)
  library(GenomeInfoDb)
  library(edgeR)
  library(rtracklayer)
  library(BiocParallel)
  library(bsseq)   # Class and tools to analyze bisulfite sequencing data at CpG-level
  library(DMRcate) # Identification of differentially-methylated regions
})
## Warning: пакет 'matrixStats' был собран под R версии 4.3.3
## Warning: пакет 'S4Vectors' был собран под R версии 4.3.2
## Warning: пакет 'GenomeInfoDb' был собран под R версии 4.3.3
## Warning: пакет 'AnnotationHub' был собран под R версии 4.3.3
## Warning: пакет 'ensembldb' был собран под R версии 4.3.3
## Warning: пакет 'GenomicFeatures' был собран под R версии 4.3.3
## Warning: пакет 'AnnotationDbi' был собран под R версии 4.3.2
## Warning: пакет 'edgeR' был собран под R версии 4.3.2
## Warning: пакет 'DMRcate' был собран под R версии 4.3.2

#Load data

setwd("C:/Users/lo/Desktop/master/bioinformatics/week8")
getwd()
list.files()
options(timeout = 6000)
download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/w11_practical.zip", "w11_practical.zip")
unzip("w11_practical.zip")
# Load data 
bs <- readRDS("ESC_astrocytes_BSseq.rds")

Data preparation

bs <- bs[rowSums(assay(bs, "Cov") > 0) == ncol(bs), ]

design <- model.matrix(~celltype, data = pData(bs))
methdesign <- modelMatrixMeth(design)


bs <- BSmooth(bs, BPPARAM=MulticoreParam(4, progress=TRUE))
## Warning in MulticoreParam(4, progress = TRUE): MulticoreParam() not supported
## on Windows, use SnowParam()

##   |                                                                              |===================================================================== |  99%  |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%
# Annotate for DMRcate
seqAnnot <- sequencing.annotate(
  bs,
  methdesign,
  all.cov = TRUE,
  coef = "celltypeastrocytes"
)
## Filtering out all CpGs where at least one sample has zero coverage...
## Processing BSseq object...
## Transforming counts...
## Fitting model...
## Your contrast returned 45364 individually significant CpGs. We recommend the default setting of pcutoff in dmrcate().

#DMR

dmrcateRes <- dmrcate(seqAnnot, C = 2, min.cpgs = 5)
## Fitting chr1...
## Fitting chr10...
## Fitting chr11...
## Fitting chr12...
## Fitting chr13...
## Fitting chr14...
## Fitting chr15...
## Fitting chr16...
## Fitting chr17...
## Fitting chr18...
## Fitting chr19...
## Fitting chr2...
## Fitting chr20...
## Fitting chr21...
## Fitting chr22...
## Fitting chr3...
## Fitting chr4...
## Fitting chr5...
## Fitting chr6...
## Fitting chr7...
## Fitting chr8...
## Fitting chr9...
## Fitting chrM...
## Fitting chrX...
## Fitting chrY...
## Demarcating regions...
## Done!
dmrRanges <- extractRanges(dmrcateRes, genome = "hg19")
## see ?DMRcatedata and browseVignettes('DMRcatedata') for documentation
## loading from cache
dmr_chr1 <- dmrRanges[seqnames(dmrRanges) == "chr1"]
dmr_chr1 <- dmr_chr1[order(abs(dmr_chr1$meandiff), decreasing = TRUE)]

Plot

top5_dmrs <- head(dmr_chr1, 5)


metDMRs <- bsseq::getMeth(
  bs,
  regions = top5_dmrs,
  what = "perRegion"
)


colnames(metDMRs) <- colnames(bs)


rownames(metDMRs) <- paste0(
  "DMR_", seq_len(nrow(metDMRs)),
  "_chr", seqnames(top5_dmrs),
  ":", start(top5_dmrs), "-", end(top5_dmrs)
)


pheatmap(
  metDMRs,
  name = "methylation"
)

Part 2

atac <- readRDS("ATAC_AstroVsESC_subset.rds")
head(atac)
## GRanges object with 6 ranges and 4 metadata columns:
##       seqnames              ranges strand |     logFC    logCPM       PValue
##          <Rle>           <IRanges>  <Rle> | <numeric> <numeric>    <numeric>
##   [1]     chr3   32911212-32911472      * | -14.53804   2.57720 1.84223e-185
##   [2]     chr8     9007982-9008485      * | -13.66711   1.65566 6.70803e-143
##   [3]     chr3   57164721-57165102      * | -15.00431   3.05803 6.19768e-137
##   [4]    chr22   46476146-46476721      * |  14.11141   1.44761 5.26376e-134
##   [5]     chr1   23763045-23763443      * | -14.54371   2.58018 1.99867e-133
##   [6]    chr12 124907086-124908139      * |   8.93254   1.68093 4.15580e-133
##                FDR
##          <numeric>
##   [1] 1.22824e-180
##   [2] 4.25568e-139
##   [3] 3.09624e-133
##   [4] 2.32832e-130
##   [5] 8.57188e-130
##   [6] 1.75564e-129
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 24 sequences from an unspecified genome; no seqlengths
head(dmrRanges)
## GRanges object with 6 ranges and 8 metadata columns:
##       seqnames              ranges strand |   no.cpgs min_smoothed_fdr
##          <Rle>           <IRanges>  <Rle> | <integer>        <numeric>
##   [1]    chr19     2250311-2253429      * |        94                0
##   [2]     chr1 240160779-240161545      * |        47                0
##   [3]    chr13 109792493-109793503      * |        79                0
##   [4]    chr22   17081986-17083542      * |        44                0
##   [5]    chr19   48945963-48947530      * |        75                0
##   [6]     chr2 225307000-225307498      * |        42                0
##           Stouffer       HMFDR       Fisher   maxdiff  meandiff
##          <numeric>   <numeric>    <numeric> <numeric> <numeric>
##   [1] 7.80388e-307 1.14587e-08  0.00000e+00 -10.42893  -6.31410
##   [2] 6.83910e-304 4.95791e-10 3.05917e-284 -11.49759  -8.85801
##   [3] 1.78283e-213 1.61001e-07 1.47131e-246  -9.34866  -5.93610
##   [4] 5.66783e-256 1.90808e-09 2.27241e-239 -10.85622  -8.32649
##   [5] 8.07702e-245 2.34736e-08 2.36146e-226 -10.35454  -6.17428
##   [6] 1.44341e-234 1.51794e-09 5.45341e-220 -11.18320  -8.15044
##         overlapping.genes
##               <character>
##   [1] AMH, MIR4321, JSRP1
##   [2]                <NA>
##   [3]               MYO16
##   [4]              TPTEP1
##   [5]              GRIN2D
##   [6]                <NA>
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 23 sequences from an unspecified genome; no seqlengths
atac_clean <- GRanges(
  seqnames = seqnames(atac),
  ranges   = ranges(atac),
  logFC    = atac$logFC,
  FDR      = atac$FDR
)

dmr_clean <- GRanges(
  seqnames = seqnames(dmrRanges),
  ranges   = ranges(dmrRanges),
  meandiff = dmrRanges$meandiff
)

hits <- findOverlaps(dmr_clean, atac_clean)


matched <- data.frame(
  meandiff = mcols(dmr_clean)$meandiff[queryHits(hits)],
  logFC    = mcols(atac_clean)$logFC[subjectHits(hits)],
  FDR      = mcols(atac_clean)$FDR[subjectHits(hits)]
)


ggplot(matched, aes(x = meandiff, y = logFC)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = FDR < 0.05), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("grey70", "steelblue"),
                      labels = c("ns", "FDR < 0.05")) +
  labs(x = "DMR mean methylation difference",
       y = "ATAC logFC",
       colour = "ATAC significance",
       title  = "Methylation vs Chromatin Accessibility") +
  theme_bw()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

cor.test(matched$meandiff, matched$logFC, method = "spearman")
## Warning in cor.test.default(matched$meandiff, matched$logFC, method =
## "spearman"): Есть совпадающие значения: не могу высчитать точное p-значение
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  matched$meandiff and matched$logFC
## S = 3.0955e+10, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##        rho 
## -0.2624789
 We overlapped differentially methylated regions (DMRs) with differential ATAC-seq peaks and compared the direction of change (methylation difference vs. log fold change in accessibility) using a scatter plot and Spearman correlation.
There is a significant negative correlation (ρ = −0.26, p < 2.2e-16), indicating that regions gaining methylation in astrocytes tend to become less accessible, and vice versa — consistent with DNA methylation being associated with closed chromatin. The modest effect size suggests this relationship holds genome-wide but is not the only regulatory mechanism at play.
